paper.title: Seventeen 2 Micron All Sky Survey (2MASS) hypervelocity stars (HVS) from Gaia DR3

extraction.summary: Thirteen HVS candidates extracted from Table 1. Four objects excluded as cluster members or YSOs.

method_chain.summary: Adopt Gaia DR3 astrometry, photometry, and radial velocities, and 2MASS source identifiers.

source_refs.context: searched the Gaia DR3 data for HVS adopting a simple search criteria
seventeen 2MASS sources with Gaia DR3 G magnitudes

method_chain.summary: Select sources with Gaia G < 16.0 and radial velocity < -600 km/s.

source_refs.context: Gaia G magnitudes less than 16.0 and radial velocities less than -600 km/sec

method_chain.summary: Adopt geometric distances from Bailer-Jones et al. (2021).

source_refs.context: The distances given in Table 1 are from Bailer-Jones et al. (2021)

method_chain.summary: Adopt radial velocities from Gaia DR3 (10 LPVs) or Jonsson et al. (2020) high-resolution spectra (5 stars).
outputs: radial velocities with typical errors ~10 km/s (Gaia) or ~1 km/s (Jonsson)

source_refs.context: For 10 LPVs the radial velocities given in Table 1 are from Gaia DR3

method_chain.summary: Calculate Galactic velocity components U, V, W and tangential velocity Vt from proper motions, distances, and radial velocities.

source_refs.context: calculated the U, V, W, and Vt (tangential velocity) velocities

method_chain.summary: Author classifies the seventeen stars as hypervelocity stars (HVS) based on their high radial velocities and calculated kinematics, without explicit bound/unbound orbit integration.

source_refs.context: These velocities and radial velocities (Table 1) clearly indicate that these seventeen 2MASS stars are HVS

method_chain.summary: Paper reports stellar parameters and spectral types from Gaia DR3, SIMBAD, or other literature without describing the original inference method.

source_refs.context: Teff, log g, and [Fe/H] values given below for some of the stars are from the Gaia DR3 data

inclusion_assessment.summary: Paper presents J17500025-1542221 as a hypervelocity star (HVS) based on Gaia DR3 kinematics with RV < -600 km/s.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper explicitly identifies this star as a hypervelocity star (HVS) in the title, abstract, and discussion text.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Thirteen 2MASS stars from Table 1 presented by the paper as hypervelocity stars without local cluster or YSO associations.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Paper explicitly identifies these stars as hypervelocity stars (HVS) in the title, abstract, and discussion text.

source_refs.context: ongoing search for hypervelocity stars (HVS) I found seventeen two micron all sky survey (2MASS) sources
These velocities and radial velocities (Table 1) clearly indicate that these seventeen 2MASS stars are HVS

candidate_groups_considered.description: Four objects from Table 1 identified in the paper as cluster members or young stellar objects.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: J23470671+6311571 and J00504461+5824384 are identified as cluster members; J02274456+6140299 and J02475329+6044285 are identified as YSOs in star-forming regions W3 and W5. These are cluster escapers or runaways, not Galactic-unbound HVS candidates per Stella criteria.

source_refs.context: may be a member of galactic cluster
red giant in an open cluster
B3-6 Ve star in star forming complex W3
B6e star in star forming region W5
